title: Quiz: Quantum Generators for QGANs
description: Test your understanding of quantum generator design and implementation

questions.question: What does a quantum generator produce?
questions.explanation: A quantum generator uses a parameterized quantum circuit U(θ) to transform |0⟩^n into quantum states |ψ(θ)⟩ that can be measured to produce samples.

questions.question: Which is NOT a common noise injection strategy for quantum generators?

options.text: Encoding noise in input rotation layer

questions.explanation: Common noise injection strategies include parameter noise, input encoding, and ancilla-based methods. Direct Hamiltonian modification is not a standard approach for noise injection in quantum generators.

questions.question: What affects the expressiveness of a quantum generator?

options.text: Number of parameters, entanglement structure, and circuit depth

questions.explanation: Quantum generator expressiveness depends on multiple factors: the number of trainable parameters, the entanglement structure (full, linear, etc.), and the circuit depth.

questions.question: What gradient method uses only two circuit evaluations per parameter?
questions.explanation: The parameter-shift rule computes exact gradients using evaluations at θ ± π/2, requiring exactly two circuit evaluations per parameter.

questions.question: Quantum generators can only output discrete binary samples, not continuous values.
questions.explanation: False. Quantum generators can output continuous values through various methods: expectation values of observables, probability amplitudes, or post-processing of measurements.

questions.question: Why might a quantum generator use linear entanglement instead of full entanglement?

options.text: Full entanglement is not possible on quantum hardware
Linear entanglement requires fewer parameters and is less prone to barren plateaus

questions.explanation: Linear entanglement uses fewer entangling gates and parameters, making training easier and less susceptible to barren plateaus, though at the cost of some expressiveness.

questions.question: What is SPSA gradient estimation used for?

options.text: Efficient approximate gradients when many parameters exist

questions.explanation: SPSA (Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation) estimates gradients with only 2 function evaluations regardless of the number of parameters, making it efficient for high-dimensional optimization.

questions.question: What can quantum generators output? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Quantum generators can output: (a) quantum states for quantum tasks, (b) binary samples via measurement, (c) probability distributions from |ψ|², and (d) continuous features via expectation values or post-processing.
